title: Predicting schistosome transmission in rural Uganda using water contact data from wearable GPS devices
methods: radius of gyration for human mobility
recursion analysis for GPS contact detection
data_sources: Wearable GPS loggers (i-gotU GT-600, Mobile Action Technology)
SchistoTrack cohort (field mapping + Kato-Katz infection data)
Open Data Kit Collect household surveys
core_conclusion: Ten days of wearable GPS data from 452 individuals in rural Uganda show that simple exponential spatial decay functions of household distance predict individual-level open-water contact (AUROC = 0.87) and tap/borehole usage (AUROC = 0.92) without needing mobility or WASH covariates. Integrating decay-based visitation rules into a spatially explicit individual-based model reproduced observed one-year S. mansoni reinfection prevalence (40.5%) and ranked water sites by transmission potential. Safe-water provision (WASH) failed to displace open-water contact even in counterfactual scenarios, with fewer than 2% of individuals modeled to fully substitute.
why_it_matters: First individual-level GPS validation of spatial decay assumptions for schistosome water-contact; challenges meta-analytic WASH effectiveness claims; produces a scalable, low-burden tool (15 individuals, 10 days) for focal intervention targeting; links micro-behavior to macro-epidemic dynamics through a biologically realistic IBM.
why_nature: Addresses 250-million-person disease burden with direct 2022 WHO guideline relevance; methodological paradigm shift from coarse gravity models to individual-level wearable GPS inference; high-impact null result (WASH crowding-out fails) with policy consequences; strong triangulation across GPS, infection, and IBM data types.
added_value_to_my_research: GPS-data-learned distance decay replaces assumed Gaussian/power-law kernels in 2SFCA/R2SFCA healthcare-access models
Spatiotemporal KDE of IBM-output site-visit counts yields a spatially varying transmission intensity surface applicable to any environmentally mediated disease
Multi-source fusion of behavioral GPS data + remote-sensing snail habitat maps via explainable ML creates an interpretable focal-risk score
Formal transportability analysis of decay parameters informs when geospatial foundation models need domain adaptation vs. direct transfer
Household-to-site bipartite water-contact network analyzed with community detection reveals exposure clusters for spatially targeted MDA
